title: Qin Shi Huang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Qin Shi Huang was the founder of the Qin dynasty and the first emperor of China.

title: Puyi becomes Emperor of China
fact: Puyi was the last emperor of China, reigning as the eleventh monarch of the Qing dynasty from 1908 to 1912, and a brief return in 1917, when he was forced to abdicate.

title: Kublai Khan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Kublai Khan, also known by his temple name as the Emperor Shizu of Yuan and his regnal name Setsen Khan, was the founder and first emperor of the Mongol-led Yuan dynasty of China.

title: Wu Zetian becomes Empress of China
fact: Empress Wu, commonly known as Wu Zetian, personal name Wu Zhao, was the only female sovereign in the history of China.
image: 唐后行從圖_(局部)_-_武則天.png

title: Qianlong Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Qianlong Emperor, also known by his temple name Emperor Gaozong of Qing, personal name Hongli, was the fifth emperor of the Qing dynasty and the fourth Qing emperor to rule over China proper.

title: Hong Xiuquan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Hong Xiuquan, born Hong Huoxiu and with the courtesy name Renkun, was a Chinese revolutionary and religious leader who led the Taiping Rebellion against the Qing dynasty.

title: Yuan Shikai becomes Emperor of China
fact: Yuan Shikai was a Chinese general and statesman.

title: Guangxu Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Guangxu Emperor, also known by his temple name Emperor Dezong of Qing, personal name Zaitian, was the tenth emperor of the Qing dynasty, and the ninth Qing emperor to rule over China proper.

title: Emperor Gaozu of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Gaozu of Han, personal name Liu Bang, was the founder and first emperor of the Han dynasty.

title: Kangxi Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Kangxi Emperor, also known by his temple name Emperor Shengzu of Qing, personal name Xuanye, was the third emperor of the Qing dynasty, and the second Qing emperor to rule over China proper.
image: 清圣祖康熙皇帝朝服像.jpg

title: Hongwu Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Hongwu Emperor, personal name Zhu Yuanzhang, was the founding emperor of the Ming dynasty, reigning from 1368 to 1398.

title: Emperor Taizong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Taizong of Tang, previously Prince of Qin, personal name Li Shimin, was the second emperor of the Tang dynasty of China, ruling from 626 to 649.

title: Yongle Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Yongle Emperor, personal name Zhu Di, was the third emperor of the Ming dynasty, reigning from 1402 to 1424.

title: Liu Bei becomes Emperor of China
fact: Liu Bei, courtesy name Xuande (玄德), was a Chinese warlord in the late Eastern Han dynasty who later became the founding emperor of Shu Han, one of the Three Kingdoms of China.

title: Yongzheng Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Yongzheng Emperor, also known by his temple name Emperor Shizong of Qing, personal name Yinzhen, was the fourth emperor of the Qing dynasty, and the third Qing emperor to rule over China proper.

title: Emperor Taizu of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Taizu of Song, personal name Zhao Kuangyin, courtesy name Yuanlang, was the founding emperor of the Song dynasty of China.

title: Xianfeng Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Xianfeng Emperor, also known by his temple name Emperor Wenzong of Qing, personal name Yizhu, was the eighth emperor of the Qing dynasty, and the seventh Qing emperor to rule over China proper.

title: Emperor Wu of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Wu of Han, personal name Liu Che and courtesy name Tong, was the seventh emperor of the Han dynasty from 141 to 87 BC.

title: Xuande Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Xuande Emperor, personal name Zhu Zhanji, was the fifth emperor of the Ming dynasty, reigning from 1425 to 1435.

title: Tongzhi Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Tongzhi Emperor, also known by his temple name Emperor Muzong of Qing, personal name Zaichun, was the ninth emperor of the Qing dynasty, and the eighth Qing emperor to rule over China proper.
image: 清_佚名_《清穆宗同治皇帝朝服像》.jpg

title: Daoguang Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Daoguang Emperor, also known by his temple name Emperor Xuanzong of Qing, personal name Minning, was the seventh emperor of the Qing dynasty, and the sixth Qing emperor to rule over China proper.

title: Shunzhi Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Shunzhi Emperor, also known by his temple name Emperor Shizu of Qing, personal name Fulin, was the second emperor of the Qing dynasty, and the first Qing emperor to rule over China proper.
image: 清世祖顺治皇帝朝服像.jpg

title: Jiaqing Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Jiaqing Emperor, also known by his temple name Emperor Renzong of Qing, personal name Yongyan, was the sixth emperor of the Qing dynasty and the fifth Qing emperor to rule over China proper.
image: 清_佚名_《清仁宗嘉庆皇帝朝服像》.jpg

title: Temür Khan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Öljeyitü Khan, born Temür, also known by his temple name as the Emperor Chengzong of Yuan, was the second emperor of the Yuan dynasty of China, ruling from 10 May 1294 to 10 February 1307.

title: Toghon Temür becomes Emperor of China
fact: Toghon Temür, also known by his temple name as Emperor Huizong of Yuan and by his posthumous name as Emperor Shun of Yuan, was the last emperor of the Yuan dynasty and the first emperor of the Northern Yuan dynasty.

title: Emperor Xuanzong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, personal name Li Longji, was an emperor of the Tang dynasty of China, reigning from 712 to 756.

title: Hong Taiji becomes Emperor of China
fact: Hong Taiji, sometimes rendered as Huang Taiji and occasionally referred to as Abahai in Western literature, also known by his temple name Emperor Taizong of Qing, was the second khan of the Later Jin dynasty and the founding emperor of the Qing dynasty.
image: 清人画皇太极朝服像轴_2418_x_3564.jpg

title: Wanli Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Wanli Emperor, personal name Zhu Yijun, was the 14th emperor of the Ming dynasty, reigning from 1572 to 1620.

title: Hongzhi Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Hongzhi Emperor, personal name Zhu Youcheng, was the tenth emperor of the Ming dynasty, reigning from 1487 to 1505.

title: Zhengtong Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Yingzong of Ming, personal name Zhu Qizhen, was the sixth and eighth emperor of the Ming dynasty.

title: Emperor Gaozu of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Gaozu of Tang, personal name Li Yuan, courtesy name Shude, Xianbei name Daye Yuan, was the founding emperor of the Tang dynasty of China, reigning from 618 to 626 CE.

title: Sun Quan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Sun Quan, courtesy name Zhongmou (仲謀), posthumously known as Emperor Da of Wu, was the founder of Eastern Wu, one of the Three Kingdoms of China.

title: Jiajing Emperor of Ming becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Jiajing Emperor, personal name Zhu Houcong, was the 12th emperor of the Ming dynasty, reigning from 1521 to 1567.

title: Emperor Ai of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Ai of Han, personal name Liu Xin, was an emperor of China's Han dynasty.

title: Wang Mang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Wang Mang, courtesy name Jujun, officially titled the Shijianguo Emperor, was the founder and the only emperor of the short-lived Xin dynasty of imperial China.
image: WUL-he21_01467_新鐫繪像東西漢全傳_01_(王莽).jpg

title: Zhengde Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Zhengde Emperor, personal name Zhu Houzhao, was the 11th emperor of the Ming dynasty, reigning from 1505 to 1521.

title: Cao Pi becomes Emperor of China
fact: Cao Pi, courtesy name Zihuan, was the first emperor of the state of Cao Wei in the Three Kingdoms period of China.

title: Emperor Xian of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Xian of Han, personal name Liu Xie (劉協), courtesy name Bohe, was the 14th and last emperor of the Eastern Han dynasty of China.

title: Li Zicheng becomes Emperor of China
fact: Li Zicheng, born Li Hongji, also known as the Thunder King, was a Chinese peasant rebel leader who helped overthrow the Ming dynasty in April 1644 and ruled over northern China briefly as the Yongchang Emperor of the short-lived Shun dynasty before his death a year later.

title: Emperor Guangwu of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Guangwu of Han, personal name Liu Xiu (劉秀), courtesy name Wenshu (文叔), was the founding emperor of the Chinese Eastern Han dynasty.

title: Emperor Wen of Sui becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Wen of Sui, personal name Yang Jian (楊堅), childhood name Naluoyan (那羅延), Xianbei name Puliuru Jian (普六茹堅), was the founding emperor of the Sui dynasty of China.

title: Hongxi Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Hongxi Emperor, personal name Zhu Gaochi, was the fourth emperor of the Ming dynasty, reigning from 1424 to 1425.

title: Emperor Taizong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Zhao Jiong, known as Zhao Guangyi from 960 to 977 and Zhao Kuangyi before 960, also known by his temple name as the Emperor Taizong of Song, was the second emperor of the Song dynasty of China.

title: Emperor Wu of Jin becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Wu of Jin, personal name Sima Yan, courtesy name Anshi (安世), was a grandson of Sima Yi, nephew of Sima Shi and son of Sima Zhao.

title: Chenghua Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Chenghua Emperor, personal name Zhu Jianshen, changed to Zhu Jianru in 1457, was the ninth emperor of the Ming dynasty, reigning from 1464 to 1487.

title: Emperor Yang of Sui becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Yang of Sui, personal name Yang Guang (楊廣), alternative name Ying (英), childhood name Amo (阿摩), Xianbei name Puliuru Guang (普六茹廣), was the second emperor of the Sui dynasty of China.

title: Emperor Zhongzong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Zhongzong of Tang, personal name Li Xian, and at other times Li Zhe or Wu Xian, was the fourth and seventh emperor of the Tang dynasty of China, ruling briefly in 684 and again from 705 to 710.

title: Taichang Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Taichang Emperor, personal name Zhu Changluo, was the 15th emperor of the Ming dynasty.

title: Emperor Wen of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Wen of Han, personal name Liu Heng (劉恆), was the fifth emperor of the Han dynasty from 180 until his death in 157 BC.

title: Külüg Khan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Külüg Khan, born Khayishan, also known by his temple name as the Emperor Wuzong of Yuan, was an emperor of the Yuan dynasty of China.

title: Emperor Hui of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Hui of Han, born Liu Ying (劉盈), was the second emperor of the Han dynasty.

title: Emperor Renzong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Renzong of Song, personal name Zhao Zhen, was the fourth emperor of the Northern Song dynasty of China.

title: Emperor Gaozong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Gaozong of Song, personal name Zhao Gou, courtesy name Deji, was the tenth emperor of the Chinese Song dynasty and the first of the Southern Song dynasty, ruling between 1127 and 1162 and retaining power as retired emperor from 1162 until his death in 1187.

title: Jayaatu Khan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Jayaatu Khan, also known by his temple name as the Emperor Wenzong of Yuan, was an emperor of the Yuan dynasty.

title: Emperor Ruizong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Ruizong of Tang, personal name Li Dan, also known at times during his life as Li Xulun, Li Lun, Wu Lun, and Wu Dan, was the fifth and ninth emperor of the Chinese Tang dynasty.

title: Ayurbarwada Buyantu Khan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Buyantu Khan, born Ayurbarwada, also known by his temple name as the Emperor Renzong of Yuan, was the fourth emperor of the Yuan dynasty.

title: Liu Shan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Liu Shan, courtesy name Gongsi, was the second and last emperor of the state of Shu Han during the Three Kingdoms period.

title: Emperor Jing of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Jing of Han, born Liu Qi, was the sixth emperor of the Han dynasty from 157 to 141 BC.

title: Emperor Bing of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Zhao Bing, also known as Emperor Bing of Song or Bing, Emperor of Song (宋帝昺), was the 18th and last emperor of the Song dynasty of China, who ruled as a minor between 6 and 7 years of age.

title: Gegeen Khan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Gegeen Khan, born Shidibala, also known by his temple name as the Emperor Yingzong of Yuan, was an emperor of the Yuan dynasty.

title: Yesün Temür Khan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Yesün Temür was a great-grandson of Kublai Khan and an emperor of the Yuan dynasty from 1323 to 1328.

title: Huang Chao becomes Emperor of China
fact: Huang Chao was a wealthy Chinese salt trader and soldier who is primarily known for instigating the Huang Chao Rebellion.
image: 黃巢（殘唐五代史演義傳）.jpg

title: Emperor Xuan of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Xuan of Han, born Liu Bingyi (劉病已), was the tenth emperor of the Han dynasty, reigning from 74 to 48 BC, and was one of the only four Western Han emperors to receive a temple name.

title: Longqing Emperor becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Longqing Emperor, personal name Zhu Zaiji, was the 13th emperor of the Ming dynasty, reigning from 1567 to 1572.

title: Emperor Hui of Jin becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Hui of Jin, personal name Sima Zhong (司馬衷), courtesy name Zhengdu (正度), was the second emperor of the Western Jin dynasty.

title: Emperor Shenzong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Shenzong of Song, personal name Zhao Xu, was the sixth emperor of the Song dynasty of China.

title: Cao Rui becomes Emperor of China
fact: Cao Rui, courtesy name Yuanzhong, was the second emperor of the state of Cao Wei during the Three Kingdoms period.

title: Rinchinbal Khan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Rinchinbal, also known by his temple name as the Emperor Ningzong of Yuan, was a son of Kuśala who was briefly installed to the throne of the Yuan dynasty of Mongol, but died soon after he was installed to the throne.

title: Emperor Daizong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Daizong of Tang, personal name Li Yu, né Li Chu, was an emperor of the Chinese Tang dynasty.

title: Emperor Suzong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Suzong of Tang, personal name Li Heng, né Li Sisheng (李嗣升), known as Li Jun (李浚) from 725 to 736, known as Li Yu (李璵) from 736 to 738, known briefly as Li Shao (李紹) in 738, was an emperor of the Chinese Tang dynasty and the son of Emperor Xuanzong.

title: Emperor Taizu of Later Liang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Taizu of Later Liang (後梁太祖), personal name Zhu Quanzhong (朱全忠), né Zhu Wen (朱溫), name later changed to Zhu Huang (朱晃), nickname Zhu San, was a Chinese military general, monarch, and politician.

title: Ragibagh Khan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Ragibagh, also known by his era name as the Tianshun Emperor of Yuan, was a son of Yesün Temür who was briefly installed to the throne of the Yuan dynasty in Shangdu in 1328.

title: Emperor Zhenzong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Zhenzong of Song, personal name Zhao Heng, was the third emperor of the Song dynasty of China.

title: Wu becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Wu of Liang, personal name Xiao Yan (蕭衍), courtesy name Shuda (叔達), childhood name Lian'er (練兒), was the founding emperor of the Chinese Liang dynasty, during the Northern and Southern dynasties period.

title: Emperor Chengdy of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Cheng of Han, personal name Liu Ao, was an emperor of the Chinese Han dynasty ruling from 33 until 7 BC.

title: Emperor Wuzong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Wuzong of Tang, né Li Chan, later changed to Li Yan just before his death, was an emperor of the Tang dynasty of China, reigning from 840 to 846.

title: Emperor Ming of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Ming of Han, courtesy name Zili, born Liu Yang and also known as Liu Zhuang and as Han Mingdi, was the second emperor of the Eastern Han dynasty.

title: Khutughtu Khan becomes Emperor of China
fact: Khutughtu Khan, also known by his temple name as the Emperor Mingzong of Yuan, was a son of Khayishan who seized the throne of the Yuan dynasty of China in 1329, but died in suspicious circumstances six months later.

title: Emperor Taizu of Jin becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Taizu of Jin, personal name Aguda, sinicised name Min, was the founder and first emperor of the Jurchen-led Jin dynasty of China.

title: Emperor Gong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Gong of Song, personal name Zhao Xian, was the 16th emperor of the Song dynasty of China and the seventh emperor of the Southern Song dynasty.

title: Emperor Yuan of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Yuan of Han, personal name Liu Shi, was an emperor of the Chinese Han dynasty.

title: Emperor Zhao of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Zhao of Han, born Liu Fuling (劉弗陵), was the eighth emperor of the Han dynasty from 87 to 74 BC.

title: Emperor Dezong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Dezong of Tang, personal name Li Kuo, was an emperor of the Chinese Tang dynasty.

title: Emperor Zhaozong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Zhaozong of Tang, né Li Jie, name later changed to Li Min and again to Li Ye, was the penultimate emperor of China's Tang dynasty.

title: Prince of Changyi becomes Emperor of China
fact: Liu He was briefly the ninth emperor of the Han dynasty.

title: Emperor Xianzong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Xianzong of Tang, personal name Li Chun, né Li Chun (李淳), was an emperor of the Chinese Tang dynasty.

title: Emperor Yingzong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Yingzong of Song, personal name Zhao Shu, was the fifth emperor of the Song dynasty of China.

title: Emperor Wu of Liu Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Wu of (Liu) Song, personal name Liu Yu (劉裕), courtesy name Dexing (德興), childhood name Jinu (寄奴), was a Chinese statesman and strategist in the late Eastern Jin dynasty, and the founding emperor of the Liu Song dynasty of China.

title: Sun Hao becomes Emperor of China
fact: Sun Hao, courtesy name Yuanzong, originally named Sun Pengzu with the courtesy name Haozong, was the fourth and last emperor of the state of Eastern Wu during the Three Kingdoms period of China.

title: Emperor Xiaowen of Northern Wei becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Xiaowen of Northern Wei ( 魏孝文帝), personal name Tuoba Hong (拓跋宏), later Yuan Hong (元宏), was an emperor of China's Northern Wei dynasty, reigning from September 20, 471 to April 26, 499.

title: Emperor Gengshi of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: The Gengshi Emperor, born Liu Xuan, was an emperor of the Han dynasty that had been restored following the downfall of Wang Mang's short-lived Xin dynasty.

title: Emperor Yuan of Jin becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Yuan of Jin, personal name Sima Rui (司馬睿), courtesy name Jingwen (景文), was an emperor of the Jin dynasty and the first emperor of the Eastern Jin.

title: Emperor Lizong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Lizong of Song, personal name Zhao Yun, was the 14th emperor of the Song dynasty of China and the fifth emperor of the Southern Song dynasty.

title: Emperor Xizong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Xizong of Tang, né Li Yan, later name changed to Li Xuan, was an emperor of China's Tang dynasty.
image: 唐僖宗帝（殘唐五代史演義傳）.jpg

title: Emperor Xiaozong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Xiaozong of Song, personal name Zhao Shen, courtesy name Yuanyong, was the 11th emperor of the Song dynasty of China and the second emperor of the Southern Song dynasty.

title: Emperor Duzong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Duzong of Song, personal name Zhao Qi, was the 15th emperor of the Song dynasty of China and the sixth emperor of the Southern Song dynasty.

title: Emperor Qinzong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Qinzong of Song, personal name Zhao Huan, was the ninth emperor of the Song dynasty of China and the last emperor of the Northern Song dynasty.

title: Emperor Zhang of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Zhang of Han, born Liu Da (劉炟), was an emperor of the Chinese Han dynasty from 75 to 88.

title: Emperor He of Han becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor He of Han was an emperor of the Chinese Han dynasty who ruled from 88 to 106.

title: Emperor Zhuangzong of Later Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Zhuangzong of Later Tang, personal name Li Cunxu, nickname Yazi (亞子), stage name Li Tianxia (李天下), was the second ruling prince of the Former Jin dynasty who later became the founding emperor of the Later Tang dynasty during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period of Chinese history.

title: Emperor Wenzong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Wenzong of Tang (809–840), personal name Li Ang, né Li Han (李涵), was an emperor of the Tang dynasty of China.

title: Emperor Duanzong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Duanzong of Song, personal name Zhao Shi, was the 17th emperor of the Song dynasty of China, and the eighth and penultimate emperor of the Southern Song dynasty.

title: Emperor Zhezong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Zhezong of Song, personal name Zhao Xu, was the seventh emperor of the Song dynasty of China.

title: Sun Liang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Sun Liang, courtesy name Ziming, was the second emperor of the state of Eastern Wu during the Three Kingdoms period of China.

title: Emperor Muzong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Muzong of Tang, personal name Li Heng, né Li You (李宥), was an emperor of the Tang dynasty of China.

title: Emperor Jingzong of Tang becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Jingzong of Tang, personal name Li Zhan, was an emperor of China's Tang dynasty.
image: 帝鉴图说.Recueil_Historique_des_Principaux_Traits_de_la_Vie_des_Empereurs_Chinois.95帧图.彩绘册页.约18世纪_(唐敬宗)_(cropped).jpg

title: Emperor Huai of Jin becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Huai of Jin, personal name Sima Chi (司馬熾), courtesy name Fengdu (豐度), was an emperor of the Jin dynasty.

title: Emperor Min of Jin becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Min of Jin, personal name Sima Ye, courtesy name Yanqi (彥旗), was an emperor of the Jin dynasty and the last of the Western Jin.

title: Emperor Guangzong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Guangzong of Song, personal name Zhao Dun, was the 12th emperor of the Song dynasty of China and the third emperor of the Southern Song dynasty.

title: Emperor Ningzong of Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Ningzong of Song, personal name Zhao Kuo, was the 13th emperor of the Song dynasty of China and the fourth emperor of the Southern Song dynasty.

title: Emperor Wen of Liu Song becomes Emperor of China
fact: Emperor Wen of Liu Song, personal name Liu Yilong (劉義隆), childhood name Che'er (車兒), was an emperor of the Liu Song dynasty of China.
